response.justification: The candidate has a broad 15+ year software engineering background, but the role's primary requirement — hands-on C++ development for industrial automation — is almost entirely missing from the profile. C++ appears only in a bachelor's thesis project and a 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and is conspicuously absent from the candidate's own skills list. There is no evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems, STL usage, MQTT, IEC 62443 secure development practices, or industrial-protocol experience, all of which are central to this position. The candidate's actual expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js web and full-stack development.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse engineering experience with demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, distributed systems (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby).
Event-driven architecture experience from GISCollective and Dunnhumby aligns loosely with the message-broker and large-scale application requirements.
Node.js and React expertise covers two of the role's listed advantageous skills.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — a thesis project and one 6-month role over a decade ago — and is not listed among the candidate's own skills.
No exposure to IEC 62443, secure development lifecycle practices, or any industrial cybersecurity concepts.
No IoT, industrial automation, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850), which are core to the role's domain.
